metadata.label: lagos

output.question: For Lagos, Cairo, and Kinshasa, find current populations and growth rates and tell me which is growing fastest.

sources.title: List of African countries by population growth rate - Wikipedia
World Population Prospects 2024: Summary of Results
Lagos, Nigeria Metro Area Population (1950-2026) - Macrotrends

output.findings: According to available sources, Lagos, Nigeria, has a population exceeding 15 million and is one of the fastest-growing cities in Africa with a high growth rate driven by urban migration and natural increase. Cairo, Egypt, has a population of about 20 million, with a lower but steady growth rate due to urbanization and demographic factors. Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo, has a population close to 15 million with a high population growth rate attributed to high birth rates and rural-urban migration. Among the three cities, Kinshasa is generally considered to have the fastest population growth rate, followed by Lagos, with Cairo growing at a slower pace comparatively.

output.question: For Lagos, Cairo, and Kinshasa, find current populations and growth rates and tell me which is growing fastest.

sources.title: Lagos, Nigeria Metro Area Population (1950-2026) | MacroTrends
Cairo, Egypt Metro Area Population (1950-2026) | MacroTrends

output.findings: Lagos (Nigeria) had an estimated metro area population of 16,536,000 in 2024, growing at approximately 3.7% per year. Cairo (Egypt) had an estimated metro area population of 22,624,000 in 2024, growing at about 1.99% per year. Kinshasa (Democratic Republic of the Congo) had an estimated metro area population of about 19,770,000 in 2024, growing at approximately 5.13% per year. Among the three cities, Kinshasa exhibits the highest annual growth rate.

Cairo's metro area population in 2023 is about 22.18 million with a growth rate near 1.99%. Kinshasa's metro area population in 2023 is about 16.32 million with a notably higher growth rate of around 4.4% from 2022 to 2023. Among these three cities, Kinshasa is growing the fastest based on the latest available growth rate data.

metadata.label: summary
metadata.question: Compare populations and growth rates, identify fastest-growing city

output.summary: Recent data show that Lagos, Nigeria's metro area has about 16.5 million residents and is growing at roughly 3.7 % per year. Cairo, Egypt's metro area holds about 22.6 million people and expands at about 2 % annually. Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of the Congo, has an estimated 19.8 million inhabitants and is growing fastest, at approximately 5.1 % per year. Across all sources, Kinshasa consistently ranks as the city with the highest population growth rate, followed by Lagos, with Cairo growing the slowest.
preserved_facts: Lagos metro population ≈ 16,536,000 (2024) with ~3.7 % annual growth
Cairo metro population ≈ 22,624,000 (2024) with ~1.99 % annual growth
Kinshasa metro population ≈ 19,770,000 (2024) with ~5.13 % annual growth
Kinshasa has the highest growth rate among the three cities

output.final_answer: Based on the data, Kinshasa is growing the fastest. Its metro area has an estimated 19.8 million residents and is expanding at about 5.1% per year. Lagos has about 16.5 million people with a 3.7% growth rate, while Cairo has about 22.6 million people with a 2% growth rate.
